questions.question: A CW visible laser has 25W power output and 2.0 mrad beam divergence. Calculate the NOHD slant range using Equation 70-1.2.
A green laser (520 nm) outputs 15W of visible power. The VCF for 520 nm is 0.7092. Calculate the Visually Corrected Power.
questions.reference: FAA AC 70-1B, Appendix A, Section A.14.4

questions.question: Calculate the SZED slant range for a laser with 10.64 W VCP, 1.5 mrad divergence, using Equation 70-1.3.
Given SZED_SR = 13,148 feet, calculate the LFED slant range.
What is the Visual Correction Factor for a 555 nm laser (peak visual sensitivity)?
questions.explanation: 555 nm represents peak photopic (day) visual sensitivity, with a VCF of 1.0000. All other wavelengths have lower VCF values.

questions.question: A laser has minimum elevation angle of 10° and maximum of 60°. The NOHD slant range is 5,000 feet. Calculate the horizontal distance.
Using the same laser (min 10°, max 60°, NOHD_SR = 5,000 feet), calculate the vertical distance.
For a single pulse laser with pulse energy of 2 J and MPE of 0.1 J/cm², what is the constant factor (inside the square root) in Equation 70-1.1?
questions.explanation: Equation 70-1.1 uses 1.2732 (4/π) as the constant factor for pulse energy calculations. Different constants are used for CW (1273.2) and SZED (12732).

questions.question: A laser has three visible wavelengths: 5W at 450 nm (VCF 0.038), 3W at 520 nm (VCF 0.709), and 2W at 630 nm (VCF 0.265). Calculate the total Visually Corrected Power.
questions.reference: FAA AC 70-1B, Appendix A, Section A.14.4.2

questions.question: What is the ratio between SZED and CZED exposure thresholds?
questions.explanation: SZED threshold is 100 µW/cm² and CZED is 5 µW/cm². The ratio is 100:5 or 20:1. The square root of this ratio (4.47) is the multiplier used in Equation 70-1.4.
questions.reference: FAA AC 70-1B, Appendix A, Equations 70-1.3 and 70-1.4

questions.question: A repetitively pulsed laser has pulse energy of 0.5 J and PRF of 100 Hz. Calculate the Pre-corrected Power for visual effect calculations.
questions.explanation: PCP = 0.5 × 100 = 50 W. For repetitively pulsed lasers, multiply pulse energy by PRF to get equivalent average power for visual effect calculations.
questions.reference: FAA AC 70-1B, Appendix A, Section A.14.4.1

questions.question: For a beam with minimum elevation of 5° and maximum of 30°, which angles should be used for horizontal and vertical distance calculations respectively?
questions.explanation: Horizontal distance uses MINIMUM elevation angle (5° - beam closest to horizontal for maximum horizontal reach). Vertical distance uses MAXIMUM elevation angle (30° - beam highest point).
questions.reference: FAA AC 70-1B, Appendix A, Section A.16.4.2
